items.title: 'We must avenge the blood of our leader': Crowds throng Tehran for Khamenei funeral ceremonies
items.desc: Waving blood-red flags seen as a call for vengeance and justice under Shia Islam, mourners thronged the Grand Mosalla religious complex.

items.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
items.desc:
Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire.

items.title: twlegalrag 1.1.0
items.desc: Open-source CLI for semantic Taiwan legal judgment retrieval — search judgments, package them for your own AI tools, and run a bundle-level citation check. Retrieval-only; does not generate or endorse legal advice.

items.title: Unamerican: Cost of average July 4th BBQ surges 31% to $295 in NYC: ‘just insane’
items.desc: The already sky-high prices of BBQ essentials jumped a staggering 31% in the Big Apple since last year, according to a review of retail prices across city grocery stores.

items.title: Pope Leo urges migrant protection, integration on visit to Lampedusa
items.desc: Pontiff urges people in Europe and US to welcome and assist migrants during symbolic trip to Italy’s frontier island.

items.title: WATCH: David Muir goes inside the vault holding America's founding documents
items.desc: On this day in 1776, the Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia. David Muir is in the room where it all happened – and sees the first draft of the U.S. Constitution.

items.title: A retired insurance executive found a lost Declaration of Independence in Britain’s National Archives
items.desc: Captured from a Revolutionary privateer in 1776, the document sat unread in Royal Navy files for 250 years until Michael Scurr unfolded it one morning.

items.title: Boston’s Aroldis Chapman sets MLB record for strikeouts as a reliever with his 1,364th K
items.desc: The strikeout was the 1,364th in Chapman’s 17-year career. It broke the record long held by Hoyt Wilhelm, the Hall of Famer whose 21-year career ended back in 1972.

items.title: Pope Leo marks July 4 at migrant graves, not U.S. celebrations
